title: Pituitary symptoms
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

presenting_history.text: Supports headache, visual loss or blurring, diplopia, cranial-neuropathy, fatigue, and altered-mental-status context as clinician-recorded symptoms or prompts.
presenting_history.rationale: Supports mapped pituitary symptom identity, headache, visual, fatigue, and acute-visual-loss prompt fields.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Supports headache, visual loss or blurring, diplopia, cranial-neuropathy, fatigue, and altered-mental-status context as clinician-recorded symptoms or prompts.

source.title: Pituitary incidentaloma: a Pituitary Society international consensus guideline statement

investigations.text: Supports pituitary imaging review, visual fields, and visual-acuity documentation in recommendation-qualified contexts.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped pituitary imaging, visual-field, and visual-acuity documentation.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports pituitary imaging review, visual fields, and visual-acuity documentation in recommendation-qualified contexts.

source.title: Pituitary incidentaloma: a Pituitary Society international consensus guideline statement

referral.text: Supports endocrinology or neurosurgery consultation documentation in qualified severe-headache, visual-deficit, hormone-symptom, lesion, or pregnancy contexts.
referral.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-arranged specialist referral documentation with qualifying features retained.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports endocrinology or neurosurgery consultation documentation in qualified severe-headache, visual-deficit, hormone-symptom, lesion, or pregnancy contexts.

source.title: Pituitary incidentaloma: a Pituitary Society international consensus guideline statement

follow_up.text: Supports pituitary hormone-profile review and relevant reproductive or prolactin symptom context without inferring hormone deficiency or excess.
follow_up.rationale: Supports mapped reproductive, galactorrhoea, and hormone-profile review fields without inferring a disorder.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports pituitary hormone-profile review and relevant reproductive or prolactin symptom context without inferring hormone deficiency or excess.

source_limitations: The broad symptoms workflow does not encode incidentaloma, adult, lesion-size, pregnancy, endocrine, or surgical qualifiers.

Seizure, neurological screen, general appearance, growth or weight change, hand or foot size, thirst or urine output, bone or joint pain, previous records, hormone replacement, generic clinician plan, follow-up, fixed intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, hormone-result interpretation, hormone replacement, treatment, surgery, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
